title: Patch test result review

investigations.text: Supports clinician-reviewed exposure history, photographs, patch testing, KOH or culture, and selected tests when diagnosis is uncertain, without recommending testing automatically.
investigations.rationale: The exact UAE DHA investigation section supports documenting why a patch-test result is being reviewed, exposure and distribution context, reviewed photographs and an already available patch-test or pathology result; it does not interpret the result automatically.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-reviewed exposure history, photographs, patch testing, KOH or culture, and selected tests when diagnosis is uncertain, without recommending testing automatically.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Allergic Contact Dermatitis

exact_section.heading: Section 9 — patch testing, laboratory tests, photographs, and histopathology

referral.text: Supports documenting trigger avoidance, skin-care or emollient discussion, and clinician-stated topical treatment without mapping medicine names, doses, frequency, duration, or autonomous advice.
referral.rationale: The management section supports recording only a clinician-entered discussion, safety net and arranged follow-up after result review; it does not create avoidance, workplace, skin-care, medicine, or referral advice.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports documenting trigger avoidance, skin-care or emollient discussion, and clinician-stated topical treatment without mapping medicine names, doses, frequency, duration, or autonomous advice.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Allergic Contact Dermatitis

exact_section.heading: Sections 11–12 — clinician-led avoidance, skin care, and treatment

follow_up.text: Supports contact-dermatitis terminology, itch, burning, erythema, scale, vesicles, blistering, edema, weeping, distribution, exposure, occupation, products, temporal course, atopy, and clinician-observed morphology.
follow_up.rationale: Exposure, occupation, product, temporal, morphology, and distribution context was reviewed for result applicability.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports contact-dermatitis terminology, itch, burning, erythema, scale, vesicles, blistering, edema, weeping, distribution, exposure, occupation, products, temporal course, atopy, and clinician-observed morphology.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Allergic Contact Dermatitis

source_limitations: Specific allergens, concentrations, controls, readings, irritant versus allergic interpretation, current exposure relevance, occupational implications, diagnosis, avoidance plan, treatment, referral, and follow-up timing remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
